summary: Lead accepts the M7 visual/UX handoff after systems_architect ACCEPT and independent qa_reviewer ACCEPT. The handoff is accepted as M7 UI/localization quality-track planning input only, not UI implementation approval, production-art approval, formal content lock, release approval, or Human Gate approval.

tests_run.result: No whitespace errors in lead-side verification before acceptance.

decisions.decision: Accept the visual/UX handoff as coordination-ready and suitable to inform downstream M7 UI/localization task graph updates.

This acceptance does not authorize UI, i18n, token, asset, app-shell, product code, package code, sim rule, content, release, telemetry, server/multiplayer, cloud-save, account, secret, production art, or manual node battle implementation.

Downstream tasks must inherit i18n outside sim-core, React/client state outside authoritative WorldState, Pixi render-cache-only, SimulationPort/GameCommand/query boundaries, player/debug separation, full Human Gate list, and DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE.
The zh-CN versus zh-Hans naming mismatch is recorded as a downstream i18n-foundation issue; no locale-code decision is approved by this handoff alone.

risks.summary: Downstream UI/localization task authors could over-read target-expression guidance as new domain-system authorization.
risks.mitigation: Derived tasks must cite existing read models/commands or classify work as layout, copy, token, asset-slot, or regression-only.

risks.summary: All derived UI/localization tasks must inherit the complete Human Gate list rather than the shortened handoff summary.
Product directive uses zh-CN while existing coverage may use zh-Hans.
risks.mitigation: A downstream M7 i18n foundation task must select or alias locale IDs through systems review and tests.

requested_action: Commit, push, open/merge PR, then close M7-VISUAL-UX-HANDOFF-001 only after the accepted commit enters main; create or update downstream M7 UI/localization tasks in a follow-up planning task before implementation.
